exploration_log.aha_moment: The existing `diff_today`, `diff_yesterday`, `diff_tomorrow` translation keys in Lang/en.php map directly to the 'today/yesterday/tomorrow' outputs. `getTranslatedDayName()` in Date.php handles the locale-aware day name for the 'last/next {day}' case. Only two new translation keys were needed for the 'last/next' pattern.
exploration_log.hindsight: Check whether there's already a 'calendar_last' translation key across Lang/ files before adding new keys; there wasn't, so adding to en.php with a fallback string is the right approach.

alternatives: Add new method to a different trait
key_decisions.reasoning: The task explicitly says to add it to the Converter trait, which hosts all to*String() formatting methods.

alternatives: Add translation keys to all 200+ locale files
key_decisions.decision: Add calendar_last_day/calendar_next_day keys only to en.php with fallback to English
key_decisions.reasoning: Modifying all locale files is out of scope; providing a fallback ensures correctness for non-translated locales without breaking change.

solve_notes: Added `toRelativeCalendarString()` to `Traits/Converter.php`. It compares the instance's `toDateString()` against the reference date (default: now) to detect today/yesterday/tomorrow, then uses `getTranslatedDayName()` plus new translation keys `calendar_last_day`/`calendar_next_day` (added to `Lang/en.php`) for dates beyond tomorrow. All comparison uses existing `toDateString()` and `resolveCarbon()` patterns already used throughout the codebase.
task_text:
Add `toRelativeCalendarString()` for colloquial date references

The `Converter` trait in `Traits/Converter.php` provides various
`toString` formats but lacks a relative calendar method that returns
"today", "yesterday", "tomorrow", "last Monday", or "next Friday"
depending on the date relative to now. Add `toRelativeCalendarString()`
using the existing locale translations from `Lang/` for the day and
relative terms.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: getTranslatedDayName
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms the method signature and that it returns a locale-aware day name; agent would search to verify it doesn't require arguments

thrash_preventing_defs.name: diff_today
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms the exact string key name and pattern for existing relative labels; prevents agent from inventing wrong key names

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set contains the three edited defs (toRelativeCalendarString, two new lang keys). The thrash_preventing set adds isToday, getTranslatedDayName, and the existing diff_today key so an AI agent understands (a) what date-comparison pattern the project already uses, (b) the exact signature of the day-name helper, and (c) the naming convention for existing locale keys — all three would be proactively searched before writing a new method of this kind.
